topic: Quantum computing applications in cryptography post-2022
final_review:
The advent of quantum computing has significant implications for cryptography, as it can potentially break certain classical encryption algorithms (Juels and Wattenberg, 1999; Lenstra and Verheul, 2001). In recent years, there has been a growing interest in post-quantum cryptography, which refers to cryptographic techniques that are resistant to attacks by quantum computers (Fernández‐Caramés and Fraga‐Lamas, 2020; Alagic et al., 2022). The integration of blockchain technology with post-quantum cryptography is also an active area of research, with potential applications in secure data storage and transmission (Fernández‐Caramés and Fraga‐Lamas, 2020; Yaga et al., 2018).

The road to post-quantum cryptography is closely tied to the development of quantum computing itself, with researchers exploring the potential of quantum computing in various fields, including 6G communications (Jiang et al., 2021; de Alwis et al., 2021). However, there are still significant challenges to be addressed, including the development of practical and efficient post-quantum cryptographic algorithms (Preskill, 2018; Shekhawat and Gupta, 2024). Post-quantum cryptography has been explored in the context of smart-grid networks (Shekhawat and Gupta, 2024) and the Internet of Things (Fernández‐Caramés, 2019).

Furthermore, the potential of blockchain technology to enable secure and efficient data transmission in 6G networks is being explored (Jiang et al., 2021; Noor‐A‐Rahim et al., 2022). Despite the significant progress made in post-quantum cryptography, there are still many open questions and research gaps (Alagic et al., 2022). For example, the development of practical and efficient post-quantum cryptographic algorithms is still an active area of research (Preskill, 2018). Additionally, the integration of post-quantum cryptography with other technologies, such as blockchain and 6G communications, is still in its early stages (Fernández‐Caramés and Fraga‐Lamas, 2020; Yaga et al., 2018).

In conclusion, post-quantum cryptography is a rapidly evolving field, with significant implications for the security of data transmission and storage. Further research is needed to address the many challenges and open questions in this area, including the development of practical and efficient post-quantum cryptographic algorithms and the integration of post-quantum cryptography with other technologies (Vaishnavi and Pillai, 2021).

References:
Alagic, G., Apon, D., & Cooper, D. A. (2022). Status report on the third round of the NIST Post-Quantum Cryptography Standardization process. https://doi.org/10.6028/nist.ir.8413
de Alwis, C., Kalla, A., & Pham, Q. V. (2021). Survey on 6G Frontiers: Trends, Applications, Requirements, Technologies and Future Research. https://doi.org/10.1109/ojcoms.2021.3071496
Fernández‐Caramés, T. M. (2019). From Pre-Quantum to Post-Quantum IoT Security: A Survey on Quantum-Resistant Cryptosystems for the Internet of Things. https://doi.org/10.1109/jiot.2019.2958788
Fernández‐Caramés, T. M., & Fraga‐Lamas, P. (2020). Towards Post-Quantum Blockchain: A Review on Blockchain Cryptography Resistant to Quantum Computing Attacks. https://doi.org/10.1109/access.2020.2968985
Jiang, W., Han, B., & Habibi, M. A. (2021). The Road Towards 6G: A Comprehensive Survey. https://doi.org/10.1109/ojcoms.2021.3057679
Juels, A., & Wattenberg, M. (1999). A fuzzy commitment scheme. https://doi.org/10.1145/319709.319714
Lenstra, A. K., & Verheul, E. R. (2001). Selecting Cryptographic Key Sizes. https://doi.org/10.1007/s00145-001-0009-4
Noor‐A‐Rahim, M., Liu, Z., & Lee, H. (2022). 6G for Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) Communications: Enabling Technologies, Challenges, and Opportunities. https://doi.org/10.1109/jproc.2022.3173031
Preskill, J. (2018). Quantum Computing in the NISQ era and beyond. https://doi.org/10.22331/q-2018-08-06-79
Shekhawat, H., & Gupta, D. (2024). A survey on lattice‐based security and authentication schemes for smart‐grid networks in the post‐quantum era. https://doi.org/10.1002/cpe.8080
Vaishnavi, A., & Pillai, S. (2021). Cybersecurity in the Quantum Era-A Study of Perceived Risks in Conventional Cryptography and Discussion on Post Quantum Methods. https://doi.org/10.1088/1742-6596/1964/4/042002
Yaga, D., Mell, P., & Roby, N. (2018). Blockchain technology overview. https://doi.org/10.6028/nist.ir.8202
